meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. An Intermediate-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for student developers.
content.answer_block: Without version control, your codebase is a ticking time bomb. By integrating robust SaaS pricing logic—like tiered subscription models—directly into your architecture, you ensure your app is monetizable and scalable. Use Antigravity to scaffold your billing schema and Claude Code to automate the deployment of your versioned repository.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect UI, but your project is a fragile house of cards. One accidental 'rm -rf' or a botched merge, and your hard work vanishes into the ether. This is the silent killer of student dreams: the absence of version control, leaving your code vulnerable and your progress untracked.

But the pivot point between a hobbyist and a professional engineer is how you handle complexity. By treating your SaaS pricing infrastructure as the first 'feature' you version control, you transform your project from a static file into a living, revenue-generating engine. Let's secure your future, one commit at a time.

steps.title: Initialize Git Versioning Strategy
steps.description: Establish a robust Git workflow to protect your core logic before writing pricing code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a standard Git repository structure with .gitignore templates for Node.js, environment variable protection, and branch protection rules.
steps.claude_code_command: claude git init && git branch -M main

steps.title: Define SaaS Pricing Architecture
steps.description: Create a modular pricing service that handles tiers, ensuring it remains decoupled from your UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean architecture for a Stripe-integrated pricing service, including types for Subscription, Tier, and Usage-based billing.

steps.title: Implement Versioned Migrations
steps.description: Ensure your database schema changes are versioned alongside your pricing logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database migration script that tracks schema versions for user subscription statuses and plan history.

steps.title: Automate Pricing Logic Testing
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your pricing tiers to prevent breaking changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write Jest test suites that validate pricing calculation logic for monthly, annual, and trial-period scenarios.

steps.title: Commit and Deploy Workflow
steps.description: Finalize your setup by pushing to a remote repository with automated CI/CD triggers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow that runs tests on every push and deploys the pricing microservice upon merging to main.

pro_tips: Always use Semantic Versioning (SemVer) for your pricing API endpoints to avoid breaking legacy client subscriptions.
Implement 'Soft Deletes' in your database for subscription records; never permanently delete billing history.
Use Feature Flags to test new pricing tiers in production without affecting existing paying users.
Store your pricing logic in a dedicated 'domain' folder, keeping it completely framework-agnostic for easier testing.
Commit your environment variable templates (.env.example) to Git, but never the actual secrets—use a vault provider.
resources: Git Best Practices for SaaS: https://git-scm.com/book
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is versioned and secured, focus on integrating a webhook listener to handle subscription status updates asynchronously.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to build a professional-grade SaaS without the fear of losing your progress. Stop relying on luck and start relying on systems. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
